meta.title: The Cow and the Tiger — Set 4
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Fill-in and sentence practice for 'The Cow and the Tiger.'

questions.question: Once there was a cow _______ Bala. Her name was Bala.
questions.explanation: 'Named' means called by a name. 'A cow named Bala' means a cow whose name was Bala.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'sad' correctly?
options: Bala was sad when the tiger caught her.
Sad the tiger let her go.
questions.explanation: 'Sad' is an adjective meaning unhappy. 'Bala was sad' — she felt unhappy. This is the correct way to use 'sad.'

questions.question: Bala's promise was: 'I will come back so you can attack me.'
questions.explanation: Bala said: 'I promise, I promise to come back. I'll come back soon. Then you can attack!' She offered to return, which shows great bravery.

questions.question: The tiger was no longer _______. He became calm after Bala returned.
questions.explanation: The tiger was 'not mad anymore' — he was no longer angry after Bala returned and proved she was honest.

questions.question: Bala went to the _______ forest every day to eat grass.
questions.explanation: Bala liked to graze in the 'shadowy forest' — the dark, shaded forest. She grazed in her 'grassy home' but the forest she visited was 'shadowy.'

questions.question: Arrange the words to form Bala's promise:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'I promise to come back.' Bala made this promise to the tiger, and she kept it!

questions.question: The tiger said he would _______ Bala — he would keep her safe from other animals.
questions.explanation: 'Protect' means to keep someone safe. The tiger promised to protect Bala — to keep her from harm.

questions.question: 'Truth' and 'promise' are both nouns (things).
questions.explanation: Yes! Both 'truth' (what is real and honest) and 'promise' (a commitment to do something) are nouns — they are things, not actions or descriptions.

questions.question: What does 'agreed' mean in the sentence 'Tiger agreed'?
questions.explanation: 'Agreed' means said yes and accepted. When 'Tiger agreed,' he said yes to Bala's request to let her go.

questions.question: Bala was _______ because her calf was safe and the tiger would not hurt her.
questions.explanation: 'Happy' means feeling full of joy and pleasure. 'Bala was very happy' at the end — she was safe and her calf was safe too.

questions.question: Sort these words — are they ADJECTIVES (describing how something is) or VERBS (action words)?
questions.explanation: 'Beautiful' and 'surprised' are adjectives (they describe how things look or feel). 'Caught,' 'graze,' and 'attack' are verbs (they are actions).

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a sentence from the story:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'You told the truth.' The tiger said this to Bala when she came back as promised.

questions.question: Which pair of vocabulary words from the story are OPPOSITES?
questions.explanation: 'Sad' and 'happy' are opposites. Bala started the story 'sad' (caught by the tiger) and ended the story 'very happy' (free and protected).

questions.question: The tiger _______ Bala — he caught her suddenly with force.
questions.explanation: 'Caught' is the past tense of 'catch.' The tiger 'caught' Bala — he grabbed her suddenly in the forest.

questions.question: In the story, Bala's promise was: she would tell the truth AND she would come back.
questions.explanation: Bala promised to come back — and she did. By returning, she showed she was telling the truth (that she would keep her promise). Both honesty and promise-keeping are shown in her actions.
